location.office_time_required: 3 days per week (Tuesdays, Thursdays, and one flex day); up to 4 weeks remote per year

secondary_archetype: Head of Product / PM Director leading a value & economics product pillar

seniority.level: Director / VP of Product (people manager of PMs and managers of PMs)
level_signals: Build and lead a high-performing Product Management team including hiring, developing, and retaining world-class PMs and managers of PMs
Proven track record of hiring, developing, and retaining world-class product managers and managers of product managers
Comp range $304,000–$418,000 base, consistent with Director/VP-level PM leadership at a late-stage startup
Operates with GM-level economic ownership across the portfolio
Aligns executive stakeholders on investment horizons and guardrails

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Exceptional product instincts – A world-class product thinker who consistently designs simple, compelling experiences that change customer behavior. Holds a high bar for quality, clarity, and usability in high-impact flows, demonstrates strong creative judgment, and makes sharp trade-offs between completeness, simplicity, and speed.

Leadership – Leads through constant change and ambiguity, keeping teams highly motivated through ups and downs. Strikes the right balance between shielding the team and creating accountability for success.

Data-driven and analytical – Can break down complex economic and operational systems into their component parts and use data and logic to accurately diagnose root causes. Demonstrates fluency in unit economics, margin drivers, and capital constraints, and uses data effectively to inform strategic decisions.

Sought after manager, mentor, and coach – Proven track record of hiring, developing, and retaining world-class product managers and managers of product managers. Past direct reports are passionate about working with them.
Growth mindset – Willing to accept feedback and seek ways to get better every day.

Relentlessly resourceful – Won't quit and is hungry to do whatever it takes to make an impact on the community. Is willing to get their hands dirty and dive into the details to help their teams overcome obstacles.

Strategic thinking and business acumen – Makes clear, well-reasoned calls to prioritize work that creates customer value and drives business impact. Synthesizes customer, financial, and operational inputs into product strategy. Strong intuition for when to pursue incremental improvements vs. taking larger swings.
Great collaborator – Builds strong relationships across functions, both technical and non-technical. Has a proven track record of aligning disparate parts of an organization.
Excellent communicator – Distills complex technical and economic decisions into simple, concise language to drive decision-making.
Kind – Demonstrates compassion and generosity towards everyone.

scope.surface: Faire's Value Pillar — Faire Source (Fulfilled by Faire), Faire Pay, Insider, shipping/risk/retailer enablement operational systems
scope.business_model: B2B wholesale marketplace (technology platform connecting independent retailers and brands globally)
primary_metrics_inferred: Adoption and penetration of value programs (Faire Source, Faire Pay, Insider)
Unit economics and margin resilience per program
Retailer loyalty and share of wallet
Per-transaction cost efficiency as GMV grows
core_responsibilities: Hire, develop, and retain a world-class team of PMs
Lead the continued evolution and scaling of Faire Source (Fulfilled by Faire), Faire Pay, and Insider
Own the performance and scalability of systems enabling ordering, shipping, risk, and retailer enablement
Reduce complexity and design intuitive, high-performing adoption pathways across the retailer journey
Manage the Value pillar as a coherent economic system balancing growth, margin, capital efficiency, and risk
Proactively surface and evaluate new programs or structural improvements
Align executive stakeholders on investment horizons and guardrails

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and highly competitive ($304K–$418K base)
Equity explicitly mentioned as additional component
Clear product surface and portfolio ownership — not vague
Well-known investors and Square founding team heritage signal organizational maturity
Role has genuine P&L / GM-level scope, attractive for senior PM leaders seeking business ownership
Explicit hybrid flexibility (3 days in-office + up to 4 weeks remote annually)
red_flags: No years-of-experience minimum stated — seniority expectations are implicit from responsibilities and comp, not explicit
Role title 'Product Leadership' is vague and does not indicate Director vs. VP level — actual level not stated
Hybrid policy requires 3 days per week in San Francisco; fully-remote candidates are ineligible
No nice-to-have qualifications listed — all listed qualifications are framed as hard requirements, creating a very high bar with no soft signals
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed anywhere in the JD

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, scope of managing managers, and GM-level ownership language; title/level not explicitly stated)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not listed as metrics in JD)
ambiguities: Role title 'Product Leadership' is generic — the actual seniority (Director vs. VP vs. Head of Product) is never stated; inferred as Director/VP level from managing managers and comp range.
All qualifications are listed in a flat 'Qualifications' section with no 'Required' vs. 'Preferred' distinction; all treated as hard requirements (severity: hard) per rule 3.
No nice-to-have or preferred qualifications are mentioned anywhere in the JD, resulting in an empty nice_to_haves array.
